strategy: Wheel Primary (CSP post-earnings) + $11/$15 Bull Call Spread — Aug 2026

wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX at 16.89 — BELOW 20 threshold. Ideal entry: wait for VIX > 20 AND post-May 28 earnings IV crush. High IV (73.8% 30-day IV, IVR 39) makes this one of the better Wheel candidates at current prices.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: Daily chart shows downtrend broken from Feb highs (~$18) and stock is now retesting breakout ~$10.50–11.00. Lower Bollinger Band estimated ~$9.00–9.50. Do NOT enter CSP now — wait for May 28 earnings, then sell CSP on any post-earnings dip toward $9.50–10.00. The retest of the broken downtrend line is the technical entry signal.

csp.annualisedYield: ~40–75% annualised on capital at risk (high IV benefit)
csp.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured. CRITICAL: Do NOT enter before May 28 earnings — IV crush risk. Enter AFTER earnings. Close any open CSP 5 days before next quarterly earnings (~August 2026).
csp.note: ⭐ HIGH CONVICTION WHEEL CANDIDATE — IV 73.8%, debt-free balance sheet ($871M cash), first-ever GAAP profit FY2026, ARR $1.85B growing 11% YoY. If assigned at $8.50–9.50, cost basis is well below analyst targets ($13.80 avg). 1–3 contracts manageable ($850–$950/contract capital at risk). Technical: broken downtrend + retest = classic wheel entry setup.

coveredCall.exitRule: Close at 50% premium. Re-sell monthly targeting $11.97 resistance level. Close before earnings.
coveredCall.note: If assigned on CSP (cost basis ~$8.50–9.50), sell CC at $11–12 — just below the $11.97 key resistance level. This allows stock to recover while collecting premium. Once stock clears $11.97 resistance, raise CC strike to $13–14.

exitRules.entryTrigger: Enter AFTER May 28 earnings IV crush has settled (wait 1–2 days post-report). Stock is retesting broken downtrend at $10.50–11.00 — enter on any dip back to that level.
exitRules.profitExit: Close at 60–90% gain OR when stock hits $12.00–12.50 (break through $11.97 resistance). Let 1 contract ride to $14–15 for 150–220% gain.
exitRules.timeStop: For Aug expiry: if stock has not moved toward $11.50+ by ~July 10 (50% of time elapsed), close the position. For Sep/Nov: time stop applies after 50% of DTE consumed.
exitRules.lossStop: Close at 40–50% loss on premium paid. Do not hold a position that has halved in value — theta and IV contraction both work against you at that point.
exitRules.earningsHardStop: Close by May 23 (5 days before May 28 earnings) — no exceptions. Re-enter AFTER the report once IV crush settles.

options.bestFor: Good option here — 79 days of buffer at close since earnings are nearby. Low cost ($140/contract).
Sweet spot — 114 days remaining at close, can hold through earnings and still have runway. Only $40 more than Aug.
Overkill for PATH — thesis should play out in 60–90 days. Overpaying by $100/contract for unneeded time.

recommendation.title: Bull Call Spread — Downtrend Broken, Retesting; Enter Post-May 28 Earnings
recommendation.contractCost: ~$100–150 (1 spread) — very low cost, can do 3–4 spreads
recommendation.entryNote: ⚠️ EARNINGS MAY 28 — enter AFTER to avoid IV crush. Technical setup is compelling: the multi-month downtrend from $18+ has been broken and the stock is retesting the breakout level (~$10.50–11.00). The $11.97 level (red horizontal line on chart) is the first key target — it was prior support and is now resistance. A clean hold above $10.50 and break through $11.97 would confirm the reversal. Fundamentals support the thesis: first-ever GAAP profitability in FY2026, debt-free, $871M cash, ARR $1.85B, 107% net revenue retention. The 33% gap to analyst average target ($13.80) provides meaningful upside. LOW COST TRADE: 1 spread is only $100–150, so 3–4 spreads = $300–600 total within budget. Needham upgraded to Buy in March citing enterprise agentic AI adoption.

exitPlan.label: Spread 1
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $12.00–12.50 (+13–18%) on clean break through $11.97 resistance

exitPlan.label: Spread 2
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $14.00–15.00 (+32–42%) on analyst target convergence or positive Q1 FY2027 report

companyOverview: UiPath is the enterprise automation leader — its platform combines Robotic Process Automation (RPA), process mining, and agentic AI to automate complex workflows across large enterprises. The company pivoted from per-robot pricing to consumption-based and per-agent SaaS licensing, which has stabilised ARR growth. FY2026 (ended Jan 31, 2026) was a landmark year: first-ever GAAP net profit ($282.3M), revenue $1.611B (+13% YoY), and ARR $1.853B (+11% YoY). The balance sheet is fortress-grade: $871M+ cash, zero long-term debt. Added to the S&P MidCap 400 in April 2026 — institutional forced buying signal. The bull thesis: enterprise AI agents need orchestration infrastructure, and UiPath's Orchestrator platform is the incumbent layer where AI agents get deployed and monitored at scale. The bear thesis: Microsoft Power Automate bundles automation near-free with M365, and hyperscalers are building their own agent orchestration layers. Stock is down 33% YTD from ~$16+ to $10.59, creating the current technical and fundamental setup.

technicalSetup.description: Multi-month downtrend from February highs (~$18) was broken in late April/early May 2026. Stock is now retesting the broken downtrend line at ~$10.50–11.00. This is a classic technical entry: old resistance (the downtrend line) becomes support on the retest.

keyLevels.note: Lower Bollinger Band zone; ideal CSP strike if stock pulls back post-earnings
Broken downtrend line now acting as support — must hold for bull thesis
Prior support turned resistance — key target for the breakout. Break above = confirmation of trend reversal
Consensus PT; 30% upside from current price
Prior consolidation range; spread sell-leg captures move to this zone

Q1 FY2027 — do NOT hold options through this; enter after
Avg PT: $13.80 (+30%); Needham upgraded to Buy March 2026
High IV — Wheel premium is attractive; spread cost is low
Index inclusion = forced institutional buying; demand support

insiderFlow.summary: Net selling under 10b5-1 plans — CEO sold at $16.61 in January (stock now at $10.59, so selling was at a higher price). Recent executive activity is predominantly RSU grants and tax withholdings. No open-market buying. Neutral signal.

transactions.note: 10b5-1 pre-planned sale at $16.61 — stock now 36% lower. Not discretionary; not a current negative signal. Sold before the YTD decline.
Received 306,748 RSUs; 79,589 withheld for taxes. Standard executive compensation.
Long-term incentive — aligns exec interests with stock recovery. Not open-market activity.

insiderFlow.signal: NEUTRAL — CEO sold at $16.61 under 10b5-1 (pre-planned, now stock is lower). No discretionary open-market selling at current depressed prices. RSU grants show execs are compensated in stock — their new grants vest at current low prices, creating natural alignment with recovery.

catalysts: Downtrend broken (technical): multi-month downtrend from $18+ broken in late April — classic retest entry at $10.50–11.00
First-ever GAAP profitability (FY2026): $282.3M net income signals business model maturation — multiple re-rating catalyst
S&P MidCap 400 inclusion (April 2026): index funds must buy; creates mechanical demand floor
Needham Buy upgrade (March 2026): enterprise agentic AI adoption cited; PATH's Orchestrator is the incumbnt deployment layer for AI agents
107% Net Revenue Retention: existing customers spending more — not churning to Microsoft Power Automate despite competitive pressure
Agentic AI pivot: IXP on Google Cloud, Databricks integration, Deloitte partnership — platform expanding into the agentic workflow market
Zero debt + $871M cash: eliminates dilution risk entirely; can fund growth and buybacks
33% gap to analyst average target ($13.80): even a mean-reversion trade has 30% upside built in
risks: Microsoft Power Automate competition: bundled near-free with M365 for mid-market; most dangerous long-term structural threat
Growth deceleration: FY2027 Q1 guidance of $397.5M revenue is cautious; analysts stay on Hold fearing growth plateau
Earnings May 28 — IV crush: do NOT hold options through this date; enter after for clean exposure
AI orchestration commoditisation: hyperscalers (AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex, Azure AI) building native agent orchestration could bypass UiPath's layer
Hold-heavy analyst consensus (17 Hold / 3 Buy): market is not in buy mode yet; needs a catalyst to shift sentiment
Stock still below all key moving averages (MA-50: $10.93, MA-200: $13.10): technically in repair mode, not confirmed uptrend yet
Low options cost = low conviction signal: $11/$15 spread costs only $100–150, which is appropriate but reflects market's low conviction on the move
dilution: NO DILUTION RISK — debt-free balance sheet, $871M cash, positive GAAP FCF. RSU-based exec comp adds modest share count creep but no acute dilution concern. This is one of the cleaner balance sheets on the watchlist.
